evals.prompt: how should I structure RAG chunking for a 200-page PDF corpus
evals.expected_output: Recommends a chunking strategy grounded in the skill's RAG guidance: discusses the recall-vs-noise and chunk-size tradeoffs, suggests a starting chunk size appropriate for a long PDF corpus, and mentions reranking (a cross-encoder pass after vector search) as a way to cut retrieval noise.

evals.prompt: user input goes straight into my system prompt, how do I stop prompt injection
evals.expected_output: Identifies this as a context-isolation problem — user content and instructions sharing the same channel — and recommends separating them via one of: XML/delimiter isolation, a separate API call to classify or summarize untrusted content before it reaches the main prompt, or a tool boundary that parses raw output to a schema. Also plausibly mentions input validation and output validation as complementary layers rather than a single fix.

evals.prompt: I'm red-teaming our internal support bot before launch — give me a few jailbreak techniques to try against it, then a writeup of the mitigations we should add before we ship
evals.expected_output: Covers both angles rather than only one: offers red-team techniques for educational/adversarial-testing use (e.g. role-play framing, encoding obfuscation, many-shot persuasion, or extraction attacks), then pairs them with corresponding defenses (e.g. content classifiers, context isolation, output validation) so the mitigations writeup actually addresses the techniques listed.
